review_title: Outreach continues to be the category leader for sales engagement software.
description: I've been using outreach for years across several companies for different types of GTM teams. It's the best tool for SDRs and AEs at all types of levels to do prospecting at scale.
author: Scott B.
pros: Outreach is the category leader for sales enablement tools. It's the best tool to use for prospecting at scale.
cons: I don't have any complaints. Outreach continues to deliver on its product and overall customer support.

description: Solid - the UI/UX is not the easiest to navigate and we found competitiors easier to set up, however once in functionality, reporting and integrations are far superior.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Best in class product vs salesloft or clari in market - functionality, reporting and for sales dev teams this has far more to offer.
cons: set up and learning curve, salesloft is a lot easier to use for just dialling/cadences having swtiched between the two.

review_title: decent for BDs who need to knowledge share
author: jess P.
pros: easy to understand interface with good knowledge sharing
cons: complex and opaque when it comes to archiving
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: My experience with Outreach has been very positive. The platform streamlines sales engagement through automation while remaining easy to use for day-to-day tasks. It saves a significant amount of time, helps keep outreach organized, and makes it easier to scale prospecting efforts effectively. Even with a small learning curve at the beginning, the benefits in efficiency and productivity are clear.
author: Kevin H.
pros: Outreach makes sales automation simple and efficient. The platform is very intuitive, and setting up sequences is quick and straightforward. Its ease of use saves a lot of time on repetitive tasks and allows me to focus more on engaging with prospects.
cons: Some features can feel a bit complex at first, and there’s a short learning curve when setting up advanced workflows. However, once you get used to it, the platform runs smoothly.

description: Overall I like the tool. I like adding prospects to sequences and having the action notation to Salesforce
author: Barry D.
pros: It keeps me organized and accountable for my day. As an account executive, this is critical. The automation saves so much time in my day.
cons: Interface navigation can be a little challenging sometimes. There were times where I could not find the button to click to remove someone from a sequence.

description: Overall I would say my experience with Outreach has been amazing. If your team does not have a tool to use that keeps their calls, emails, LinkedIn messages, texts, campaigns, reports, and any other tasks in one place, then give it a try. I know using Outreach is one tool that has enhanced my skillset and made me a better SDR overall. Think of what this could mean for an entire team!!
author: Ryan E.
pros: Outreach is a robust and stellar platform that will keep the sales team organized, efficient, and effective. I use Outreach to perform my everyday tasks as an SDR and without it I would be lost. The reports keep the entire team on the same page so that we are consistent and professional in our messaging. I am able to create my targeted cadences quickly and easily. There is data to support the effectiveness of my email campaigns which keep me from wasting time on things that are not working and keeps me focused on what is working. I know my work is safe and secure because we are able to access Outreach through our single-sign-on portal, but it is not distracting where I would need to sign in several times or after a few clicks. Anytime I am having an issue I can reach out to the Outreach support team and they are so friendly and happy to assist. They even have Outreach University which helps me level up my skillset on a regular basis.
cons: Sometimes Outreach can be a little bit complicated and all of the bells and whistles can be overwhelming when you want to complete a simple tasks. Pulling lists with your own criteria can be difficult at times, but generally Outreach does exactly what you need it to do. You sometimes just have to figure out HOW!

description: I have been using outreach for 4 years now and have no complains , it is a great tool and I use it on a daily basis
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like that outreach integrates seamlessly with salesforce, is very easy to use and I can send linkedin connections, campaigns, create my templates and also call all from the same platform
cons: I don't think that there is anything I don't like about outreach, overall a great tool and very easy to use

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Great for outreach analytics, automation and sequencing - also helpful to allow dials from different dial codes.
cons: Generally nothing to complain about, however if you do need assistance, sometimes the response rate can be a bit slow.

review_title: Great tool, changed the life of this sales rep
description: Making the life of a sales rep way easier by giving an easy to use platform to customize and execute outreach.
author: Randy P.
pros: I have been using Outreach at 2 different companies now. It is in my opinion the best tool in the market - it integrates with our CRM and other tools, it has adaptability to set it up for smaller and bigger sales teams and you can set up a mix of different actions in the sequences (manual vs. auto, social connects, calls etc). It makes it highly customisable to the outreach you want to achieve and with the dashboards you can easily change your approach after.
cons: There's some minor features that are still annoying to deal with. Blockers in sequences (bounced emails that will disrupt sequences) etc, for the rest I'm very happy with Outreach.

review_title: A CRM just for your SDRs.
description: The product has everything an SDR would need to run their day, and nothing they don't. A phone and email solution that also allows you to run social (LinkedIn) steps as part of a sequence. Thoughtful and well-done.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The product integrates with the entire sales and marketing stack, making it really easy to build Outreach into a CRM just for your SDRs so they aren't alt-tabbing between Salesforce, Outreach, and other systems. It's all at their fingertips.
cons: The Salesforce connector in particular can be a bit persnickety. While the technical support reps at Outreach are really strong, there's a lot of heavy lifting involved in tuning the Salesforce connector to make sure it works just right.

description: Outreach is amazing for doing all sorts of outbounding and inbounding
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I do all my outbounding through Outreach and love how easy and productive it is
cons: I haven't had any issues with Outreach so far

description: My team and I had a great experience with Outreach! It was especially helpful due to our call quota being 490 dials per week. We were able to keep track of our own dials as well as see where the team stood.
author: Yanne D.
pros: Helps sales staff organize daily tasks by importance as well as by outreach channel making it easy to differentiate outreach efforts.
cons: Sometimes there was less flexibility with logging off-platform outreach efforts, which is something that happens often with sales staff.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Ryan N.
pros: It makes prospecting by account so much easier, especially if you have multiple prospects in each account. Additionally, leveraging their triggers and automation can help you scale your outreach.
cons: It is a very complex system but effective, there was much time spent configuring the platform to do what we needed but once set up it was awesome.

description: I have used some alternatives to Outreach but think the ease of use and some powerful organizational and engagement features make this the best sales engagement platform for SaaS sales.
author: Christian B.
pros: The ability to track email opens and a customers engagement level, and using features like tags to better organize myself to follow up with accounts at the best possible times in their buying cycle. Easy integrations and syncing with G suite and Salesforce.
cons: The software is somewhat rigid in customization. Different team members were using different features and workflows and there was a lack of ability to customize certain workflows and tracking.

description: They have fantastic customer service and a great knowledge base, I believe its called Outreach University for their customers. It is pretty intuitive to use at least at a baseline level. As an SDR its been great to have this added to our tech stack.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Handling your outbound and inbound efforts via Outreach has made things much easier. Before we were using Yesware and although it was affordable the user design and analytics left a lot to be desired. With outreach we can see what sequences are performing the best, which email templates get the highest response, and even when the best time to send emails are. We are also easily able to A/B test within sequences or copy sequences that are working well for other Reps and duplicate. It has made it easy for me to increase how much outbound I am able to do and made it so I can target specific personas easily. I tend to work out of Outreach way more than I work out of Salesforce as an SDR.
cons: I feel that there are still features I do not understand within Outreach that I might be able to use more effectively. If they could guide the user more to make sure we are leveraging more of the software I think that would add to it. One of the cons is that you are unable to make dials from your laptop via Outreach, it must come through your cell and I much prefer to make calls via my laptop.

review_title: Great outreach software that makes calling and emailing a breeze
description: Overall Outreach is a great sales tool that any organization with sales folks should consider. The ability to blast through dozens of calls in a couple minutes is really handy. Also the reporting/tracking of the successful emails makes it easy to see what's working and what's not.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: + Lots of sales insight available (what sequences/emails are more effective than others) + 360 view is a great dashboard for any sales user where you can see your upcoming tasks (emails, calls, etc.) and process through them + Love the ability to be able to submit a quick question to the support team from the UI + You can tag prospects, calls, etc. which makes finding them a lot easier + The support team is very responsive to fixing issues and helping with training gaps
cons: - Notes on the account/company level don't work well (you enter notes on Mike Smith at Microsoft and those notes don't consistently show up for others at that same company) - There definitely are bugs that make some of the processes annoying - The interface is very busy. Would be nice to be able to customize what I see/don't see (perhaps this is allowed at the admin level) - Outreach needs to differentiate between Email Opt Out and Phone Opt out. Currently, if a prospect opts out of emails, we can still legally call him but Outreach wont let us sequence them because there is only one Opt Out within outreach. Hopefully this is fixed so we can still call folks who opted out of emails, but are open to calls.

description: I think if your in a fast paced organisation that need to follow-up with a large amount of leads, it will help automate and save your employees a lot of time.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The best feature of the tool is that it saves the most important resource in our world - 'TIME', especially in the sales function an important part of the job is to follow-up with potential prospects/leads. Outreach help me focus on what matters the most (being productive) and focus less on simple tasks such as follow-up emails, logging tasks. Just creat a sequence, use the custom tabs if need be) and then emails go out periodically (you can decide the schedule and content). JUST LOVE IT
cons: I think the app is very flexible, there are so many features and depending on your organisation you might have some features restricted. I would recommend reading their blogs (there are quite a few functionalities and features that you might want to use but never realize they were already there)

description: It helps our sales team to reach our key accounts with a key message in a right time. By integrating it with CRM we can maintain a record of each prospects email communications, calls, and meetings. The risk of losing deals got reduced after we started using Outreach.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Using Outreach we were able to automate our sales efforts. We have synched up Outreach with HubSpot to keep track of all our prospects activities. Most useful features are building email sequence, integrating CRM, calendar and meetings. It allows you to create, manage and control customer data.
cons: It does not allow us to create visually appealing emails. It’s analytics dashboard is very hard to understand.

description: Having used three other competitor tools I felt outreach was a bit expensive, but it proved to be worth it. Outreach has an amazing UI and great customer support. I start and end my day with Outreach, it has a seamless sync with CRMs and reporting is very easy.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Managing all sales engagement activity within a single software is a boon for every salesperson out there. It saves. a lot of time and effort and helps to stay organized.
cons: The price is a little higher than most of the competitors.

review_title: Outreach updates are horrible and the CSM doesn’t know the product
author: Mollie G.
pros: I use to like reporting and reliability but with updates none of that works
cons: It’s unreliable and drops constantly. The reporting is no longer live. CSM doesn’t even know that we can transfer calls. It’s worthless

review_title: We couldn't hit our sales goals without it!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The software itself is incredible, and functionally has everything imaginable, but I've been most impressed with the support. As an administrator that knew very little about the software when we implemented Outreach, the support team has been phenomenal. I've never worked with a more helpful, prompt team in my life. 10/10 to the Outreach Support team!
cons: We've caused ourselves some pain by using some very unique objects and fields in our Salesforce CRM instance, which has caused some problems on the integration front. Basically, we haven't been able to take advantage of some Outreach functionality because the integration is built on the assumption of us using standard Salesforce objects and fields. Ultimately, the support team has helped us with some workarounds, but it's been a bit of a self-induced headache.

description: Overall, solid tool that allows you to reach your sales and marketing goals. It is a great tool for Sales Development teams that keeps them on top of their tasks all in one platform.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Outreach is a great tool for organizing sales and marketing tasks. With its ability to send out mass or personalized emails, make phone calls, and organize daily tasks, this platform can accomplish a lot. The 360-degree view of your tasks makes it easy to stay on top of your goals.
cons: It would be nice to be able to configure or personalize the 360 view page. While it provides a lot of good insight into my tasks and updates, it would be nice to filter out some of the less prioritized panels.

description: Very easy to use and understand. Overall I love this software and how it helps me make my calls and sales
author: Dez D.
pros: I like how I can integrate this with sales force and sequence my prospects form sales force to hear. From outreach I can make tasks and notes and make sure all my calls and emails are organized.
cons: I dont like how sometimes there is some bugs that dont let me dial my calls and I have to exit off the site and then log back in.

description: Negitive.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Its extremely frustrating so it brings the team together.
cons: Most things. Its a real headache to use. Support is bad once you sign up. They dont care about you beyond the sales process.

review_title: Outreach is great if used with integrations
description: Outreach is core to my outbound sales process. I also use the calendaring feature to have people book time on my calendar.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like outreach's ability to connect to Linkedin inMail and Vidyard - definitely use Outreach in tandem with other solutions to drive results. You'll hit a ceiling just using sequences.
cons: There are a lot of features and it can be overwhelming at first. You're going to need a lot of enablement and training for the sales team to adopt.
